name: IStateStore
description: Contract for workflow state persistence implementations. Provides checkpoint/restore capability for durable execution, allowing workflows to survive process restarts by persisting their execution state.

methods.name: save

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution

params.name: state
params.description: Serializable state dictionary containing workflow checkpoint data

methods.description: Persist the workflow execution state. Overwrites any previously saved state for the same workflow_id. The state dict must be JSON-serializable.

methods.name: load

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution to restore

methods.description: Load previously persisted workflow state. Returns null if no state exists for the given workflow_id (first execution or after deletion).

methods.name: delete

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution to clean up

methods.description: Remove persisted state for a workflow. Called after successful workflow completion to clean up. Idempotent — deleting non-existent state is a no-op.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
All methods are async — storage backends may involve I/O operations
State dict must be JSON-serializable (no custom objects, no circular references)
save() must be atomic — partial writes must not corrupt existing state
load() must return null (not raise) for unknown workflow_ids
delete() must be idempotent — deleting non-existent state must not raise
Concurrent save/load for the same workflow_id must be safe (last-write-wins acceptable)
State size is implementation-bounded — implementations may reject oversized state dicts
